portfolio.name: Lee Seoung Hyun
portfolio.intro: I am a developer who constantly thinks about efficiency. I automate repetitive tasks and simplify complex structures. Unable to end the day without solving problems, my mind continues to asynchronously compute solutions even after work.

codeit.description:
Intensively studied frontend development for 6 months at Codeit Sprint FE Bootcamp.
Developed collaboration experience and problem-solving skills through real projects using various tools and technologies.

data.growthJourney:
- During high school, I focused on learning while contemplating my career path, cultivating consistency and self-direction.
- In university, I experienced the joy of communication and knowledge sharing through presentation activities.
- At a VR theme park, I observed how planning and programs impact user experience in the field, sparking my interest in user-centered development and starting my frontend development journey.
- Through a bootcamp, I learned and applied frontend technologies while developing problem-solving skills.
- In professional work, I'm growing both my technical and communication capabilities.

details: Reduced test execution time by 67.7% relative to test count through parallel test processing using Dynamic metrics and Playwright Sharding
Fully migrated existing ESLint + Prettier-based lint automation to Biome, reducing average CI time by 98% and improving overall development speed
Developed custom onboarding guide component and improved personalized copy, achieving 42% increase in new user payment calendar entry rate and 17% increase in mobile revisit rate
Improved search efficiency through structured metadata addition and streamlined management through internal documentation
Identified missing metadata issues per page and abstracted into common logic to streamline management
Improved responsive image component with higher quality images, reducing CLS from 0.99 to 0.1 and LCP from 1.62 to 0.55
Actively used the performance tab to precisely identify areas requiring memoization and resolved unnecessary flickering
Documented issues with the existing server action-based communication architecture and established team-wide API architecture standards

Reduced bundle size from 302kB to 143kB (73.5% reduction) by removing unnecessary modules and using dynamic import

aboutMe.title: About Me
paragraphs: I keep [B]computing solutions asynchronously[/B] until a problem is fully resolved — after work, while sleeping, in the middle of the night. When something is unclear, I design an experiment or dig into the source directly.

I don't sit on what I find. I believe [B]documentation and sharing is the most effective form of communication[/B]. I've written up analyses that changed my team's technical direction, and filed open source issues from findings along the way. A personal discovery becoming a team-level change is the kind of impact I find most rewarding.
I fix what bothers me — and [B]document it so others don't have to rediscover it[/B]. I don't just solve things privately; I write it down and connect the dots.

skills.title: Skills
items: I've designed and built SSR and server component-based services with [B]Next.js App Router[/B]. I use TypeScript as a design tool — eliminating impossible states at the type level with [B]Discriminated Unions[/B], sharing server and client schemas with [B]Zod[/B] to reduce runtime errors. I manage server state with [B]Tanstack Query[/B] and handle SEO infrastructure directly.

I write [B]Playwright E2E[/B] tests and design CI environments from the ground up. I've built parallel test execution using [B]Dynamic metric and Sharding[/B] to cut test times, and designed domain-separated API mocking environments with [B]MSW[/B]. Improving the lint and build pipeline for developer productivity is something I actively take on.

I've optimized performance across bundle, image, and runtime layers — [B]CRA-to-Vite migration[/B], [B]Docker multi-stage builds[/B], [B]Web Worker[/B] and react-window virtualization, dynamic import. Each improvement is measurable. I've also handled security work directly, including [B]CSP policy compliance[/B] — which required building a custom editor module to stay within policy.

When the problem calls for it, I go beyond web frontend. I've built a [B]Turborepo[/B]-based monorepo admin system and shipped a macOS widget via [B]Homebrew[/B] using [B]Electron[/B]. Working across these layers gives me a broader range of problems I can find and solve, and lets me make decisions that aren't constrained by a single technology.

allra.summary: Fintech startup providing early payment (선정산) services to online marketplace sellers. Responsible for frontend development of the customer-facing service and internal admin system, alongside developer environment improvements.

pwc.summary: Tech subsidiary of Samil PwC (Big4), handling digital transformation of accounting and consulting workflows. Responsible for frontend development across web services and design system.

allra.title: Allra
allra.subtitle: Allra Fintech
allra.description: Pre-settlement financial service development for e-commerce sellers

details.text: Configured parallel test environment with [B]Dynamic metric[/B] and [B]Playwright Sharding[/B], reducing average test time by [B]67.7%[/B] ([L:https://team.allrafintech.co.kr/frontend-playwright-e2e-test-ci-optimization-d94b082509d8]blog[/L])
Replaced [B]ESLint + Prettier[/B] lint pipeline with [B]Biome[/B], reducing average CI lint time by [B]98%[/B]

Led [B]Datadog → OpenReplay[/B] cost-saving migration. Debugged Next.js group route sourcemap upload errors, configured CI/CD pipeline, and discovered missing handleError during source analysis, contributing via [L:https://github.com/openreplay/openreplay/issues/4476]open source PR[/L]
Analyzed Chrome CPP source to identify autocomplete ignore cause, resolved with [B]Honeypot decoy field approach to bypass autofill[/B]
Refactored pre-settlement request logic with [B]TypeScript Discriminated Union[/B], enforcing impossible states at type level to prevent runtime errors and improve readability
[B]Server Action version skew errors[/B] and queue execution limits [L:/posts/2026-01-29]analyzed and documented[/L], [B]proposing tRPC migration that initiated team-wide adoption[/B]
Unified all page OG/Twitter metadata into shared utils, introduced [B]Schema.org JSON-LD structured data[/B] (Organization, FAQ, Article)
Existing editor incompatible with in-house [B]CSP policy[/B] → migrated to react-quill-new and built a custom image resize module to resolve security violations
